mnemonics: ANOVA - Analyze Variance Over Numerous Attributes
DESIGN - Define, Experiment, Specify, Group, Inputs, Nullify
keyPoints: ANOVA tests for statistically significant differences between group means.
The F-statistic is the ratio of between-group variance to within-group variance.
Experimental design principles (like randomization, replication, and blocking) are crucial for obtaining valid results.
commonMistakes: Assuming ANOVA proves groups are identical if it fails to reject the null hypothesis (it only indicates a lack of evidence for a difference).
Conducting multiple t-tests instead of a single ANOVA, which increases the chance of a Type I error.
Failing to check the assumptions of ANOVA (normality, homogeneity of variance, independence of observations).
